claims.evidence_text: The temperature favorable for anthesis ranges from 12°C to 22°C in wheat (Farooq et al., 2011).
claims.claim_summary: Temperature favorable for wheat anthesis ranges from 12°C to 22°C.

claims.evidence_text: Pollen dysontogenesis, even abortion, has been reported while exposure to temperatures ≥30°C at this stage. ... Temperature above 30°C during anthesis affects pollen cell and microspore resulting into complete male sterility
claims.claim_summary: Temperatures ≥30°C during pollen development/anthesis cause pollen abortion and can lead to complete male sterility in wheat.

claims.evidence_text: Even 42°C for 2 h at the anthesis stage could reduce the pollen viability as well as growth of the pollen tip (Kumar et al., 2014).
claims.claim_summary: 42°C for 2 h at anthesis reduces wheat pollen viability and pollen tube growth.

claims.evidence_text: Heat stress often occurred during reproduction from flowering to final maturation... Pollen development is most sensitive to heat. ... The heat during anthesis is fatal for grain setting through disturbing reproductive success
claims.claim_summary: Wheat reproductive stages from flowering through grain filling are the most heat-sensitive; pollen development and anthesis are the most sensitive of all.

claims.evidence_text: high temperature accelerates the GFR, but shortens the GFD; however, under 30°C, the reduced GFD cannot be compensated by high GFR to enhance growth rate (Yang et al., 2014).
claims.claim_summary: At grain-fill temperatures around 30°C, the accelerated grain-filling rate cannot compensate for the shortened grain-filling duration in wheat.

claims.evidence_text: an increase of 5.4°C above normal temperature reduces the GFD by 8 days in wheat (Tiwari et al., 2012).
claims.claim_summary: A 5.4°C rise above normal temperature shortens wheat grain-filling duration by about 8 days.

claims.evidence_text: Overexpression of the rice soluble starch synthase I (SSI) gene in transgenic wheat can improve wheat productivity under terminal heat stress, with the increase in photosynthetic duration and 1,000 grain weight by 21–34% in T2 and T3 transgenic plants
claims.claim_summary: Transgenic SSI overexpression increased wheat 1,000-grain weight by 21–34% under terminal heat stress versus controls.

claims.evidence_text: When wheat plants were exposed to high temperature for 20 h, pollen mother cells (PMCs) exposed to 35°C were less likely to progress than those exposed to 30°C, and grain number per spike was reduced at 30°C, and further at 35°C
claims.claim_summary: Heat at 30–35°C around meiosis/anthesis reduces wheat grain number per spike, with greater reduction at 35°C than 30°C.
